text: The Aurora deposit is hosted in Archean basalt and controlled by the North Fault. Zircon U-Pb dating gives an age of 2.70 Ga for the basalt.

relations.head: Aurora deposit
relations.evidence: The Aurora deposit is hosted in Archean basalt

relations.head: Archean basalt
relations.evidence: gives an age of 2.70 Ga for the basalt

relations.head: 2.70 Ga
relations.evidence: Zircon U-Pb dating gives an age of 2.70 Ga

text: Orebody A at Aurora contains massive sulfide lenses within the Kora Formation. Sample AU-01 from Orebody A was analyzed by LA-ICP-MS and yielded a Cu grade of 1.2 wt%.

relations.head: Aurora Orebody A
relations.evidence: Orebody A ... within the Kora Formation

relations.head: Aurora Orebody A
relations.evidence: yielded a Cu grade of 1.2 wt%

text: The Borealis deposit occurs in the Kora Belt and is genetically related to a mafic-ultramafic intrusion. The same belt hosts the Aurora deposit.

entities.evidence: genetically related to a mafic-ultramafic intrusion

relations.head: Borealis deposit
relations.evidence: Borealis deposit occurs in the Kora Belt

relations.head: Borealis deposit
relations.evidence: genetically related to a mafic-ultramafic intrusion

relations.head: Aurora deposit
relations.evidence: The same belt hosts the Aurora deposit
